mechanism_narrative: A downward change in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and lower equity risk premia, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month when inflation is not accelerating. The signal is confirmed if rate cuts are followed by positive 20-day index returns and rejected if cuts coincide with broad risk-off selling.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX returns after FEDFUNDS declines are not above baseline when CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or falling, or if the signal's hit rate stays below 55% in a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects labor-market deterioration that reduces expected earnings growth and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also slowing, and it fails if unemployment rises while equities still rally persistently.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3pp+ 3-month UNRATE rise do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 20-day return in those episodes is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts accelerate, they signal stronger construction demand, household balance-sheet support, and cyclical growth momentum that translate into positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter. The mechanism fails if the housing lift is driven by isolated supply rebuilding without broader demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above the 6-month average are followed by non-positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the 60-day mean return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial output and improving corporate operating leverage, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if production gains occur alongside repeated equity weakness, implying the market is pricing margin compression instead of growth.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO changes fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS identifies broadening nominal demand with job creation, which should increase forward earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next month. The effect is interaction-driven and fails if retail sales rise without payroll support or payrolls grow while sales contract.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform each series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if interaction-triggered returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL continues to decelerate, the market interprets policy as restrictive relative to inflation, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days. The mechanism fails if high policy rates are absorbed without equity drawdown.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation trend is falling
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered set does not deliver negative 5-day SPX returns more often than 60% of the time, or if the average 5-day return is non-negative despite restrictive policy and falling inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates weakening labor income growth and slower consumption, which should reduce earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over about one quarter. The signal is strongest in broad employment slowdowns and fails if payroll declines are quickly reversed without market damage.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS momentum does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of episodes, or if the 60-day hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, accelerating HOUST together with rising RSAFS marks a demand rebound that improves cyclicals and broad market sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days. The regime dependence is essential; the signal fails in late-cycle overheating when the same combination is interpreted as inflationary pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST-plus-RSAFS combination does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in post-shock recovery regimes, or if its sign becomes non-positive in the same regime across a 2-year rolling window.
